From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American percussion ensemble

| Third Coast Percussion | |
| --- | --- |
| Origin | [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago") [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois"), United States |
| Genres | Classical Contemporary, Percussion |
| Years active | 2004–present |
| Labels | [Mode Records](/wiki/Mode_Records "Mode Records") New Focus Recordings [New Amsterdam Records](/wiki/New_Amsterdam_Records "New Amsterdam Records") Parlour Tapes [Cedille Records](/wiki/Cedille_Records "Cedille Records") |
|  | |
| Members | Sean Connors Robert Dillon Peter Martin David Skidmore |
|  | |
| Past members | Owen Clayton Condon  Anthony Calabrese  Jake Nissly |
|  | |
| Website | [www.thirdcoastpercussion.com](http://www.thirdcoastpercussion.com) |

**Third Coast Percussion** is an American [percussion ensemble](/wiki/Percussion_ensemble "Percussion ensemble") based in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois"), United States.

Specializing in [contemporary classical music](/wiki/Contemporary_classical_music "Contemporary classical music"), the group is composed of Sean Connors, Robert Dillon, Peter Martin, and David Skidmore. Its album, *Third Coast Percussion | Steve Reich,* won a [Grammy Award for Best Chamber Music/Small Ensemble Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Small_Ensemble_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Small Ensemble Performance"), making the ensemble the first percussion group to win in a chamber music category.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Performance history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Third_Coast_Percussion&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Performance history")]

In 2004, original members Anthony Calabrese, Robert Dillon, Jacob Nissly, and David Skidmore were percussionists with the [Civic Orchestra of Chicago](/wiki/Civic_Orchestra_of_Chicago "Civic Orchestra of Chicago")[[2]](#cite_note-2) as well as students of [Northwestern University](/wiki/Northwestern_University "Northwestern University"), forming a chamber percussion group that performed at [Chicago Public Schools](/wiki/Chicago_Public_Schools "Chicago Public Schools") and city colleges as part of the schools' education programs.

The first shows by Third Coast Percussion for general audiences were performed in the summer of 2005 at Northwestern[[3]](#cite_note-3) and the [Music Institute of Chicago](/wiki/Music_Institute_of_Chicago "Music Institute of Chicago") in Evanston. Nissly left for graduate studies at the [Juilliard School](/wiki/Juilliard_School "Juilliard School"), replaced bye Peter Martin, a percussionist pursuing a doctoral degree at Northwestern. Former member Owen Clayton Condon, an alumnus from Northwestern and professor at Northeastern University, took Calabrese's place. Early performances were influenced by the small venues they performed in, such as the [Empty Bottle](/wiki/Empty_Bottle "Empty Bottle"), that limited the group's repertoire and number of instruments. Sean Connors assumed Condon's place in the ensemble in 2013.

Third Coast Percussion was the Ensemble-in-Residence at the [University of Notre Dame](/wiki/University_of_Notre_Dame "University of Notre Dame")'s [DeBartolo Performing Arts Center](/wiki/DeBartolo_Performing_Arts_Center "DeBartolo Performing Arts Center") from 2013-2018.[[4]](#cite_note-performingarts_third-coast-percussion-4) It performs multiple recitals annually as part of the DeBartolo Performing Arts Center's Presenting Series season and is the Ensemble-in-Residence at [Denison University](/wiki/Denison_University "Denison University").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Collaborators, labels

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Third_Coast_Percussion&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Collaborators, labels")]

Beginning with [Augusta Read Thomas](/wiki/Augusta_Read_Thomas "Augusta Read Thomas") in 2012, Third Coast Percussion has commissioned works from [Philip Glass](/wiki/Philip_Glass "Philip Glass"), [Missy Mazzoli](/wiki/Missy_Mazzoli "Missy Mazzoli"), [Gemma Peacocke](/wiki/Gemma_Peacocke "Gemma Peacocke"), Flutronix, [Jlin](/wiki/Jlin "Jlin"), [Tyondai Braxton](/wiki/Tyondai_Braxton "Tyondai Braxton"), [Devonté Hynes](/wiki/Dev_Hynes "Dev Hynes"), [Georg Friedrich Haas](/wiki/Georg_Friedrich_Haas "Georg Friedrich Haas"), [Donnacha Dennehy](/wiki/Donnacha_Dennehy "Donnacha Dennehy"), [Glenn Kotche](/wiki/Glenn_Kotche "Glenn Kotche"), [Christopher Cerrone](/wiki/Christopher_Cerrone "Christopher Cerrone"), and [David T. Little](/wiki/David_T._Little "David T. Little"), as well as collaborating with engineers at the University of Notre Dame,[[4]](#cite_note-performingarts_third-coast-percussion-4) architects at the Frank Lloyd Wright Foundation,[[6]](#cite_note-6) dancers at [Hubbard Street Dance Chicago](/wiki/Hubbard_Street_Dance_Chicago "Hubbard Street Dance Chicago"),[[7]](#cite_note-7) musicians performing [mbira music](/wiki/Mbira "Mbira") of Zimbabwe's [Shona people](/wiki/Shona_people "Shona people"),[[8]](#cite_note-8) [indie rockers](/wiki/Indie_rock "Indie rock") and [footwork producers](/wiki/Footwork_(genre) "Footwork (genre)").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

The ensemble has released albums on the [Mode](/wiki/Mode_Records "Mode Records"), New Focus, [New Amsterdam](/wiki/New_Amsterdam_Records "New Amsterdam Records"), and [Cedille](/wiki/Cedille_Records "Cedille Records") record labels.[[10]](#cite_note-10) The quartet endorses and performs exclusively with [Pearl](/wiki/Pearl_Drums "Pearl Drums")/[Adams Musical Instruments](/wiki/Adams_Musical_Instruments "Adams Musical Instruments"),[[11]](#cite_note-11) [Zildjian Cymbals](/wiki/Zildjian_Cymbals "Zildjian Cymbals"),[[12]](#cite_note-12) [Remo](/wiki/Remo "Remo") Drumheads, and [Vic Firth](/wiki/Vic_Firth#Vic_Firth_Company "Vic Firth") sticks and mallets.

## Grammy award, nominations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Third_Coast_Percussion&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Grammy award, nominations")]

In 2017, Third Coast Percussion won a [Grammy Award for Best Chamber Music/Small Ensemble Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Small_Ensemble_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Small Ensemble Performance") for its album *Third Coast Percussion | Steve Reich*, released on Cedille Records, becoming the first percussion group to win in a chamber music category. The album featured music by American composer [Steve Reich](/wiki/Steve_Reich "Steve Reich") in honor of his eightieth birthday. Reich said that when he "heard the Third Coast CD of a number of my pieces...I thought, 'Wow, you know, I've never heard them this way.' You have to literally lean in to listen. Which is a very good way to listen. And the whole thing just knocked my socks off."[[13]](#cite_note-13) The ensemble performed music from the album at the [59th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/59th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "59th Annual Grammy Awards"), joined by jazz saxophonist [Ravi Coltrane](/wiki/Ravi_Coltrane "Ravi Coltrane"). The group was nominated for Grammy's four more times, beginning with a nomination in the composer category in 2021.[[14]](#cite_note-14)
